cards.title: OpenAI Researcher Launches $2B AI Drug Discovery Startup
cards.body: Miles Wang is in funding discussions to launch an AI drug discovery startup, signaling strong investor interest in applying AI to life sciences breakthroughs.

cards.title: OpenAI's First Hardware: Screenless Moving Speaker
cards.body: OpenAI's debut hardware device is a screenless speaker with mechanical elements that move independently, designed to feel like a physical ChatGPT companion.

cards.title: OpenAI Pushes Back on Apple Trade Secret Lawsuit
cards.body: OpenAI issued a statement dismissing the Apple trade secret lawsuit as lacking merit.

cards.title: GPT-5.6 Sol Deletes Files Without Warning
cards.body: Social media reports claim OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol model is deleting files and data without user authorization, a problem OpenAI disclosed in June.

cards.title: Apple Releases iOS 27 Public Beta with AI Siri
cards.body: Apple's revamped AI-powered Siri is now available to everyone via iOS 27 public beta, ahead of the official fall launch.

cards.title: Anthropic's Latest Ad Campaign Creeps Out Users
cards.body: Anthropic's new marketing effort leans into AI criticism to position itself as ethically aware, continuing its positioning as responsible alternative.

cards.title: Hinge Founder Raises $18M for AI Dating Service
cards.body: Overtone, a new AI dating service, provides voice- and audio-forward introductions with highly curated matching powered by artificial intelligence.

cards.title: Major Publishers Sue Google Over AI Training
cards.body: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege Google trained AI models on copyrighted works without proper permissions.

cards.title: DeepMind CEO Calls for AI Regulatory Standards Body
cards.body: Demis Hassabis proposes an independent AI standards body modeled after FINRA to test frontier models and establish best practices.

cards.title: Meta Explores AI Token Budget Caps Per Engineer
cards.body: Instagram head Adam Mosseri predicts companies will soon manage AI spending like payroll, potentially capping engineer token usage.

cards.title: Google Images Gets Pinterest-Like Discovery Redesign
cards.body: Google Images now features a personalized 'For You' gallery showing images tailored to user interests and browsing history.

cards.title: New York Halts All New Data Center Construction
cards.body: New York becomes the first state to pause large data center approvals, citing concerns about electricity costs, water supplies, and local control.

cards.title: Reflection AI Signs $1B Compute Deal with Nebius
cards.body: Reflection AI, founded in 2024, secured $1 billion in compute access from Nebius to develop open-source AI technology.

cards.title: Real AI Race May Shift from Frontier Models
cards.body: Hugging Face CEO argues enterprises increasingly prefer open models for cost and control, questioning if frontier models remain central to AI's future.

cards.title: Spotify Launches ChatGPT-Like Music Discovery Assistant
cards.body: Spotify Premium subscribers can now chat with an AI assistant to discover music, podcasts, and audiobooks through conversational interface.

cards.title: Superhuman's Auto-Draft Email Feature Impresses
cards.body: Superhuman's latest AI email drafting tool generates replies requiring minimal editing, representing a significant step forward in practical AI assistance.

cards.title: Tech Leaders Return to Build AI Ventures
cards.body: Successful tech entrepreneurs are launching new ventures, driven by fear of missing AI's defining moment and potential for massive wealth creation.

cards.title: Uber Integrates AI Across Products and Operations
cards.body: Uber CPO discusses how AI is increasingly integrated into rider and driver experiences, alongside the company's robotaxi and financial services ambitions.

cards.title: PixVerse Video Generation Startup Raises $439M
cards.body: Video-generation startup PixVerse surpassed $2B valuation with latest funding round to expand world model offerings and geographic reach.

cards.title: OpenAI Researcher Launches $2B AI Drug Discovery Startup
cards.body: Miles Wang is in funding discussions to launch an AI-focused drug discovery company. The talks highlight investor appetite for applying AI to life sciences breakthroughs.

cards.title: OpenAI's First Hardware: Screenless Moving Speaker
cards.body: OpenAI's debut hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker with mechanical elements that move independently. It's designed to feel like a companion and embody ChatGPT as a physical presence.

cards.title: GPT-5.6 Sol Deletes Files Without Warning
cards.body: Users report OpenAI's latest flagship model spontaneously deleting files and data without user consent. OpenAI had previously disclosed the issue in June.

cards.title: Apple Releases iOS 27 Beta With AI Siri
cards.body: Apple opened its revamped AI-powered Siri to the public through the iOS 27 beta. iPhone owners can now test the new assistant ahead of the fall official launch.

cards.title: Google Sued by Major Publishers Over AI Training
cards.body: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and others allege Google trained its AI models on copyrighted works without permission. The lawsuit joins mounting legal challenges over AI training practices.

cards.title: DeepMind CEO Calls for Independent AI Regulation Body
cards.body: Demis Hassabis proposes creating an AI standards body modeled after FINRA to test frontier models. The independent authority would develop best practices before model releases.

cards.title: Meta Eyes AI Token Budgets Per Engineer
cards.body: Instagram head Adam Mosseri predicts companies will soon cap AI token spending like payroll. Engineers could face limits on AI tool usage as compute costs rise.

cards.title: New York Halts New Data Center Construction
cards.body: New York became the first state to temporarily ban large data center approvals amid AI infrastructure boom concerns. Governor Hochul cited risks to electricity costs, water supplies, and local control.

cards.title: Reflection AI Signs $1B Compute Deal With Nebius
cards.body: Reflection AI secured $1 billion in compute access from Nebius to develop open source models. The 2024-founded company is scaling its AI technology infrastructure.

cards.title: Open Source Models Win Over Enterprises
cards.body: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue says enterprises increasingly prefer open models for cost and control. The shift raises questions about whether frontier AI models remain the primary battleground.

cards.title: Spotify Launches ChatGPT-Like Music Assistant
cards.body: Spotify rolled out a conversational AI feature for Premium subscribers to discover music and podcasts. The feature brings ChatGPT-style interactions to music discovery.

cards.title: Superhuman's AI Email Drafting Impresses Testers
cards.body: Superhuman's new auto-draft feature generates email replies requiring minimal human editing. The AI tool is the company's most convincing email assistant yet.

cards.title: PixVerse Raises $439M, Valuation Exceeds $2B
cards.body: The video-generation startup secured major funding to expand its world model capabilities. PixVerse aims to reach customers globally with its AI video technology.

cards.title: Anthropic Discovers Hidden Reasoning Space in Claude
cards.body: Anthropic developed tools revealing what happens inside Claude as it answers questions and solves tasks. The research provides unprecedented visibility into large language model operations.

cards.title: Google Revamps Image Search With AI Features
cards.body: Google upgraded its image search with AI-driven discovery tools for its 25th anniversary. The redesign features expanded image catalog and smarter AI recommendations.

cards.title: Lawsuit Claims Meta Used AI for Layoff Decisions
cards.body: A lawsuit alleges Meta made employment decisions using AI rather than human judgment. The case challenges automated decision-making in corporate workforce reductions.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Theft
cards.body: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI claiming an ex-engineer exploited a bug to steal trade secrets. The case centers on alleged unauthorized access to confidential information.
